subject: An ordinary object and the global system behind it
central_question: How did an ordinary object become the visible endpoint of an invisible global system?
viewer_promise: The viewer will understand the hidden chain of incentives, infrastructure and decisions behind a familiar object.
surprise: The object matters less than the system that made it cheap and universal.
stakes: The same system shapes labor, trade, design and environmental cost.
target_audience: English-speaking general knowledge and documentary viewers
demand_evidence: Comparable system-explainer documentaries show durable interest.
competition: Existing videos focus mainly on manufacturing rather than the full incentive system.
differentiation: Connects manufacturing, logistics, economics and behavior in one causal narrative.
research_feasibility: High: official statistics, company histories and academic research are available.
visual_feasibility: High: factories, maps, objects, diagrams and archival-style reconstructions.
risk_notes: Avoid unsupported claims about specific companies.
kill_conditions: No credible evidence for the proposed hidden causal chain.
